seo.title: Delaware Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in Delaware — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license delaware, dental assistant certification delaware, RDA delaware, DANB CDA delaware, dental assistant radiography delaware

board.name: Delaware Board of Dentistry and Dental Hygiene
board.mailing_address: Cannon Building, Suite 203, 861 Silver Lake Blvd, Dover, DE 19904

license_types.title: Dental Radiologic Technologist
license_types.description: The primary credential for dental assistants in Delaware.

steps.title: Register on DELPROS
steps.description: Access the DELPROS portal at delpros.delaware.gov and register for a new user account. Follow the prompts to create your login credentials, verify your email address, and set up security questions.

steps.title: Start Your Application
steps.description: Log into your DELPROS account, select 'Apply for a License,' then choose the 'Board of Dental Examiners' and initiate the 'Dental Assistant' application.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the Dental Assistant application within DELPROS, including personal information, educational background (high school diploma/GED), and any relevant work history.

steps.title: Upload Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Electronically upload all necessary documents, which must include proof of your high school diploma or GED and current Basic Life Support (BLS) or Cardiopulmonary Resuscitation (CPR) certification from the American Heart Association or American Red Cross.

steps.title: Pay the Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee of $69 directly through the DELPROS portal using a credit or debit card.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly log back into your DELPROS account to check the status of your application. Respond promptly to any requests for additional information or clarification from the Delaware Board of Dental Examiners.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Dental assistants can apply for Dental Radiologic Technologist through the Delaware Board of Dentistry and Dental Hygiene by registration.

faqs.question: Does Delaware require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: Yes, Delaware requires Dental Radiologic Technologist through the state board.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Delaware?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Delaware.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Delaware?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Delaware?
faqs.answer: State application fees are $250. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

reciprocity.notes: Dental assistants can apply for Dental Radiologic Technologist through the Delaware Board of Dentistry and Dental Hygiene by registration.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in Delaware
hero.dek: To apply for a Dental Assistant license in Delaware, navigate to the DELPROS (Delaware Professional Regulation Online Services) portal to create an account and submit your online application.

official_links.registration_steps: Go to delpros.delaware.gov, click 'Register a New User' to create an account, verify your email, and set up security questions. Once registered, log in to access the application options.
